codeReview.summary: Commit ae5f99a still does not satisfy the requested isolated catalog/simple/search/global scope. While it adds the intended VAL-CATALOG regression guards, it also introduces unrelated VAL-EXT negative-path assertions in the same feature diff, so the original blocking scoping issue remains.

issues.description: The rerun commit still adds `preserves exchange, treasury, and onchain contract guards for VAL-EXT negative-path assertions`, which exercises `/exchanges/*`, `/public_treasury/*`, and `/onchain/*` routes unrelated to this feature's catalog/simple/search/global scope. That means the commit is still not an auditable isolated diff for `catalog-simple-contract-regression-guards`, so the prior scrutiny failure remains unresolved.

summary: Reviewed the prior failing scrutiny report, the worker handoff/transcript, runtime-refactor-worker guidance, and commit ae5f99a. The fix improves VAL-CATALOG coverage, but it still bundles unrelated VAL-EXT assertions into `tests/invalid-params.test.ts`, so the feature commit remains out of scope and the original blocking issue is not fixed.
